info.name: Foreside Beer Case
info.description:
Beer ordering API — microservices with gRPC.

Import both environments (local + aws) alongside this collection.
Select the active environment from the top-right dropdown in Postman.

Endpoints:
- GET  /health       — confirm gateway is running
- GET  /beers        — returns the full beer menu (12 beers)
- POST /order        — place an order; streams back beer_ready events as each beer finishes, then order_complete

request.description: Confirms the gateway is running. Returns {"status":"ok"}.
Returns all 12 available beers with their preparation time, volume, and pour time.

Orders 1x Pilsner Urquell (5s total) and 2x Heineken (7s total).

Expected stream:
  t=5s  → beer_ready: Pilsner Urquell
  t=7s  → beer_ready: Heineken
  t=7s  → order_complete (total_beers: 3)

Response is newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON). Each line is a separate event.

item.name: Place Order — Mixed speed (shows concurrent pouring)

request.description:
Orders 1x Hoegaarden (12s), 1x Pilsner Urquell (5s), 1x Guinness (21s).

Expected stream — events arrive out of request order, fastest first:
  t=5s  → beer_ready: Pilsner Urquell
  t=12s → beer_ready: Hoegaarden
  t=21s → beer_ready: Guinness
  t=21s → order_complete

This demonstrates the concurrent pouring logic.
Quantity 99 exceeds the maximum of 20 per beer. Gateway should reject with 400 Bad Request before reaching any microservice.
